//: Single source of truth for v1 → v2 API compatibility. Consumed by BOTH the classifier (scan + api-scan validator) and the port prompt, so the agent and the validators can never disagree about what is allowed. Statuses: supported | renamed | caveat | stub | removed. Derived from the real v2 SDK surface (scripts/kit-sdk.ts, SDK_VERSION 0.2.0); update this file when the SDK grows.

env.note: v1-compatible: env(key, { hint, placeholder, secret }) and env(key, 'prompt text') both work — keep them verbatim. Masking is also auto-inferred from key names containing secret/password/token/key.

textarea.note: v2 has no textarea(); editor() is the multi-line input.

dev.note: dev(obj) opened DevTools in v1; inspect(obj) shows the data in v2.

toast.note: hud(message) for transient in-launcher feedback; notify() for OS Notification Center.

kenvPath.note: The workspace moved from ~/.kenv to ~/.scriptkit and the layout changed: scripts live in plugins/<plugin>/scripts. kenvPath('scripts', ...) → skPath('plugins', 'main', 'scripts', ...); other kenvPath uses usually map to skPath(...).

menu.note: menu() exists in v2 but is currently a no-op (no menu-bar icon control). Remove the call or leave it — it will not error.

setStatus.note: setStatus() exists in v2 but is currently a no-op. Prefer hud() for user-visible status.

widget.note: widget() sends a message the v2 app does not render yet ('[SDK] widget() is not yet implemented'). Rebuild the surface with div(html, actions) — use setActions()/onTab-style choices for interactivity — or split the flow into arg/select steps. If the widget was a passive always-on display, state that it has no v2 equivalent yet in behavior_changes.

vite.note: v1 vite() widget windows have no v2 equivalent. Rebuild as div()/form() flows, or declare the loss in behavior_changes.

clipboard.writeHTML.note: v2 clipboard implements only readText/writeText/readImage/writeImage. For HTML, write the plain-text fallback via clipboard.writeText or shell out to osascript/pbcopy with HTML flavor; declare the fidelity loss.

clipboard.writeBuffer.note: Not implemented in v2 (only readText/writeText/readImage/writeImage). Images: clipboard.writeImage(buffer); other flavors need a shell fallback.

clipboard.writeFileUrl.note: Not implemented in v2 clipboard; shell out (osascript 'set the clipboard to POSIX file ...') if load-bearing.

clipboard.readHTML.note: Not implemented in v2 clipboard (only readText/writeText/readImage/writeImage); use readText and declare the fidelity loss.

clipboard.readRTF.note: Not implemented in v2 clipboard; use readText and declare the fidelity loss.

clipboard.writeRTF.note: Not implemented in v2 clipboard; write plain text instead and declare the loss.

clipboard.clear.note: Not implemented in v2 clipboard; clipboard.writeText('') approximates it.

keyboard.type.note: keyboard.type() throws UnsupportedSdkFeatureError in v2 (no native key injection yet). To insert text into the frontmost app use setSelectedText(text) — it pastes at the cursor. There is no v2 way to type into arbitrary apps key-by-key.

keyboard.tap.note: keyboard.tap() throws UnsupportedSdkFeatureError in v2. Key-combo injection into other apps has no v2 equivalent; if it targeted Script Kit's own prompt, restructure with submit()/setInput().

keyboard.pressKey.note: Throws UnsupportedSdkFeatureError in v2; see keyboard.tap.

keyboard.releaseKey.note: Throws UnsupportedSdkFeatureError in v2; see keyboard.tap.

mouse.move.note: mouse.* throws UnsupportedSdkFeatureError in v2 — no coordinate mouse control.

mouse.leftClick.note: mouse.* throws UnsupportedSdkFeatureError in v2 — no coordinate mouse control.

mouse.rightClick.note: mouse.* throws UnsupportedSdkFeatureError in v2 — no coordinate mouse control.

mouse.setPosition.note: mouse.* throws UnsupportedSdkFeatureError in v2 — no coordinate mouse control.

db.note: v1 db() (lowdb) is gone. Persist plain JSON under skPath('db', '<name>.json').

store.note: v1 store() is gone; use the same JSON-file pattern as db().

exec.note: v1 exec() (execa) is gone. v2 scripts run under bun: use await Bun.$`cmd args`.text() (import { $ } from 'bun'), or node:child_process for node compatibility.

spawn.note: Import spawn explicitly from node:child_process; it is no longer a global.

get.note: v1 get/post/put/patch/del (axios) are gone. Use native fetch; note axios responses put payload on `.data`, fetch needs `await res.json()`.

setLoading.note: v1 setLoading(true/false) spinner has no v2 equivalent. Use hud() for transient feedback or omit, and declare the lost busy indicator in behavior_changes when the work is slow.

trash.replacement: node:fs rm or Finder trash via osascript
trash.note: No global trash(). For real move-to-Trash use osascript ('tell application "Finder" to delete POSIX file ...'); for plain deletion use node:fs rm.

replace.note: v1 replace() (replace-in-file) is gone; read the file, String.replace, write it back.

chalk.note: Terminal coloring is rarely meaningful in v2 prompts; drop it or import chalk explicitly (bun auto-installs imports).

highlight.note: v1 highlight() is gone; md() renders markdown (including fenced code) for div().

npm.note: v1 npm('pkg') is gone. Use a normal `import x from 'pkg'` — v2 scripts run under bun, which auto-installs imports on first run.

wait.note: No global wait(); inline a setTimeout promise.

formatDate.note: date-fns is no longer bundled; import it explicitly (bun auto-installs) or use Intl.

createChoiceSearch.note: Pass plain choice arrays to arg()/select(); v2's launcher does the filtering.

groupChoices.note: Choice grouping helpers are gone; use flat choice arrays (a `group` field on choices is not rendered by v2 yet — declare in behavior_changes if grouping mattered).

selectFile.note: Use await path() for file selection.

selectFolder.note: Use await path() and pick a directory.

registerShortcut.note: Runtime shortcut registration is gone; declare a `// Shortcut: cmd shift x` metadata comment on a dedicated script instead.

onTab.note: v1 tab pages are gone. Restructure as a top-level arg()/select() whose choice routes to each former tab's flow, or split into separate scripts.

setAlwaysOnTop.note: Window pinning is app-managed in v2; remove the call.

setIgnoreMouseEvents.note: No v2 equivalent; remove the call.

startDrag.note: No v2 equivalent; remove the call and declare in behavior_changes if the drag was load-bearing.

mainScript.note: v1 mainScript() returned to the main menu; in v2 just exit() — the launcher is already there.

getActiveScreen.note: Use getDisplays() and pick the active one.
